Let $L$ and $R$ be binary predicates that stand for:

$L$: '... is to the left of ---'
$R$: '... is to the right of ---'

We will assume that $L$ and $R$ are orders and that:

$$ \forall x(\forall y((x\neq y)\imp (Lxy\iff\neg Rxy)))$$

This means that for any two distinct objects in the domain, either the first is
to the left of the second, or the first is to the right of the second but not
both. So, every object in the domain is positioned somewhere in a line from
left to right.

a. Define the following propertoes and relations (you may use the identity
predicate if need be):

    $B^3$: '... is between --- and \_\_\_'

    $R^1$: '... is right-most'

    $L^1$: '... is left-most'

    $I$: '... is immediately to the right of ---'

b. Write down sentences expressing the following claims. You may use the properties
and relations that you defined in part (a):

    There is someone two places to the right of John.

    There are exactly two people between John and Amy.

    John is one person away from the left of the line.
